title: Please shorten below content: Following a discussion with the teaching assistant, the team determined the importance and…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please shorten below content: Following a discussion with the teaching assistant, the team determined the importance and priority of each factor based on the following steps. Firstly, the team evaluated the potential impact of each factor on multiple metrics, including revenue, profitability, growth, etc. Then evaluating the likelihood of each factor being realized. Afterward, the team quantified the impact of each factor and the probability of it occurring. It scored each factor according to its importance to the business. Based on the scores, priority was given to the most significant factors likely to occur, which have a high impact on the business. Finally, the prioritized factors were linked to the real business by identifying how each factor directly affects the company’s growth, revenue, or profitability. An action plan was also developed to address negative factors and capitalize on positive trends.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The team assessed factors based on their potential impact on revenue, growth, and profitability. They evaluated the likelihood of each factor, quantified its impact and scored it for importance. Priority was given to significant factors with high impact on the business, and an action plan was created to address negative factors and capitalize on positive trends.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Assignment Question
Explore how a company executes various price and non-price strategies in an attempt to establish and maintain a competitive advantage in the market-as-a-game. In so doing, illustrate the inter-dependencies via an action-reaction sequence of events. Support your answer with applications to real companies.

Assignment Objective
Your essay should critically assess the value-added of transaction cost economics and game theory tools. You should also use some case studies and your Workshop case research. A key objective is to outline the overlap between micro-economics and game theoretic concepts introduced in this Module. Using academic literature and coupled with the practical case research undertaken by your group during the Workshop or coupled with the research you may have undertaken in support of your individual Workshop Alternate Assignment, illustrate how the transaction cost economics and the economics
of game theory can be used to reshape strategy. So, please do use real case study materials where appropriate.

Hint: The best way to approach the assignment is to imagine that the essay is to be read by senior management. Their concerns may have to do with (i) market entry (ii) product launch (iii) challenges of technology or (iv) prices. So, proceed to analyse the market-asa-game, identify and filter the competitive threat, assess the action-reaction sequence of events and evaluate your results. And always provide a risk assessment of the likely competitive threats facing a company on choice of strategy.

Remember: You are an MBA consultant. Here is an opportunity to reflect on both the research undertaken by you at the Workshops and on the learning obtained in the preparation of your assignments for this Module. In any consultancy, you must try to impress upon the team. One way to do that is to convince them of the value-added of applying economic concepts and economic reasoning from game theory and transactions cost economics to an evaluation of their company strategy. This essay also supports the learnings in your various Practicals undertaken throughout the module.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Assignment Question:
The essay aims to explore how companies use price and non-price strategies to establish and maintain a competitive advantage in the market-as-a-game. The goal is to illustrate the inter-dependencies via an action-reaction sequence of events and assess the value-added of transaction cost economics and game theory tools. The essay should include real case studies and support from academic literature to showcase how these tools can reshape strategy.

Objective:
As an MBA consultant, the essay aims to reflect on the research undertaken at the Workshops and the learning obtained in the preparation of the Module's assignments. The goal is to impress senior management of the value-added of applying economic concepts and reasoning from game theory and transactions cost economics to evaluate their company's strategy. The essay should provide a risk assessment of likely competitive threats and support the learnings in the various Practicals undertaken throughout the module.

Hint:
The essay should analyze the market-as-a-game, identify and filter the competitive threat, assess the action-reaction sequence of events, and evaluate the results. It should reflect on market entry, product launch, challenges of technology, or prices. Real-life case studies should be used to explain the inter-dependencies of price and non-price strategies in establishing and maintaining a competitive edge.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The essay delves into how companies execute different price and non-price strategies to establish and uphold a competitive advantage in the market-as-a-game. It highlights the inter-dependencies via an action-reaction sequence of events and utilizes real companies as case studies to support the argument. Moreover, the essay critically assesses the value-added of game theory tools and transaction cost economics and outlines the overlap between the two concepts.

The essay considers market entry, product launch, technology challenges, and prices as prime concerns of senior management. It analyzes the market-as-a-game and identifies the competitive threat while filtering it to evaluate the results. Further, the essay assesses the action-reaction sequence of events while also providing a risk assessment of potential dangers when choosing a strategy.

Using academic literature and practical case research, the essay demonstrates how the economics of game theory and transaction cost economics could be used to revamp business strategies. The research group's case research workshop could also serve as a reference to illustrate how these two concepts can be imperative tools in strategy formulation and its generalization to real companies.

Overall, the essay aims to impress upon senior management the significance of incorporating economic concepts and reasoning from game theory and transaction cost economics in shaping company strategies.
